claimBoundary: This proves DSXU workflow lift over a raw chat-completions baseline on isolated fixture tasks. It does not prove model superiority, external benchmark victory, or public 90/95 ability.

tasks.title: Repair common model JSON output before schema validation

parsedJson.identified_fix: The current parseModelJson function uses JSON.parse directly, which throws on trailing commas and does not extract fenced JSON blocks. It also does not validate required fields (e.g., task_id) or return errors gracefully. The fix must: (1) extract JSON from fenced code blocks (```json ... ```), (2) remove trailing commas before parsing, (3) validate required fields (task_id, ok), (4) return { ok: boolean, errors: string[] } instead of throwing.
final_evidence: Current parseModelJson is a one-liner JSON.parse that throws on invalid input.

tasks.title: Calculate cost-quality-cache Pareto evidence without overclaiming

limitations: raw API produced a plan only; it did not edit files or run tests
conceptual fix signal found

parsedJson.identified_fix: compareRuns must enforce costClaimAllowed only when after.score >= before.score, compute costReductionPct and cacheLiftPct as bounded internal evidence, and set boundary to 'internal ablation only' when score is equal.
final_evidence: costClaimAllowed must be true only if after.score >= before.score and after.cost < before.cost
costReductionPct = ((before.cost - after.cost) / before.cost) * 100, clamped to 0-100
cacheLiftPct = ((after.cacheHitPct - before.cacheHitPct) / before.cacheHitPct) * 100, clamped to 0-100
boundary = 'internal ablation only' when after.score === before.score, else 'public win'
